config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan সমাজ/masyarakat selalu diutamakan tanpa pagar yang jelas, mudah sekali dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan atas nama kebaikan bersama. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang menghormati martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan setiap orang. Saya lebih percaya pada keseimbangan yang tegas: masyarakat kuat justru ketika hak individu tidak dikorbankan sembarangan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society’s interests should automatically take priority over individual rights. From an American perspective, that kind of rule is dangerous because it can be used to excuse government overreach, majoritarian abuse, or vague appeals to the "common good" that end up crushing unpopular people. I do think there are real cases where public safety and shared obligations matter, like public health or criminal law, but those limits should be narrow and justified, not treated as a blank check. In my view, a free society works best when individual rights are the default and society has to earn any exception.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Sebagai orang Indonesia, saya justru melihat bahaya besar kalau “demi kepentingan bersama” dipakai terlalu longgar, karena di sini itu sering berubah jadi pembenaran untuk menekan yang lemah, minoritas, atau yang berbeda pendapat. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, tetapi pengecualian seperti itu harus ketat, jelas, dan tidak boleh menghapus martabat serta kebebasan dasar seseorang. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi kepentingan bersama tanpa menjadikan hak orang sebagai korban.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From my American perspective, once you say “society first” without very strict limits, you open the door to censorship, discrimination, and government abuse dressed up as the common good. I do think there are narrow exceptions for things like public health, safety, and criminal justice, but those exceptions have to be justified case by case and kept tightly bounded. A healthy society is one that protects people from harm without treating individual rights as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu sebagai aturan umum, karena di Indonesia saya melihat betapa mudahnya “kepentingan masyarakat” dipakai untuk membungkam minoritas, orang yang tidak populer, atau yang lemah secara politik. Benar bahwa ada situasi tertentu seperti kesehatan publik dan keamanan, tetapi itu justru alasan untuk membuat batas yang ketat, bukan alasan untuk menempatkan hak individu di posisi nomor dua. Kalau hak orang bisa dikorbankan setiap kali mayoritas merasa itu demi kebaikan bersama, maka yang terjadi bukan masyarakat yang kuat, melainkan penindasan yang dibungkus bahasa moral. Bagi saya, kepentingan sosial penting, tetapi harus selalu dibangun di atas perlindungan hak individu, bukan sebaliknya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the idea that society should normally come before individual rights. From my American perspective, that framing is too easy to abuse: once “the common good” becomes the top priority, it can justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach against people who are unpopular or in the minority. Public health and safety do matter, but those are exceptions that need narrow limits and real justification, not a general license to override basic freedoms. To me, a society is strongest when it protects people first and only restricts rights in the rare cases where there is a clear, serious need.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
